verses.greek: Παῦλος ἀπόστολος ⸂Χριστοῦ Ἰησοῦ⸃ κατ’ ἐπιταγὴν θεοῦ σωτῆρος ἡμῶν καὶ ⸂Χριστοῦ Ἰησοῦ⸃ τῆς ἐλπίδος ἡμῶν
verses.mistral: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the command of God our Savior and of Christ Jesus our hope,
verses.kjv: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the commandment of God our Saviour, and Lord Jesus Christ, which is our hope;

verses.greek: Τιμοθέῳ γνησίῳ τέκνῳ ἐν πίστει· χάρις, ἔλεος, εἰρήνη ἀπὸ θεοῦ ⸀πατρὸς καὶ Χριστοῦ Ἰησοῦ τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν.
verses.mistral: Grace, mercy, peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ our Savior.
verses.kjv: Unto Timothy, my own son in the faith: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.

verses.greek: Καθὼς παρεκάλεσά σε προσμεῖναι ἐν Ἐφέσῳ, πορευόμενος εἰς Μακεδονίαν, ἵνα παραγγείλῃς τισὶν μὴ ἑτεροδιδασκαλεῖν
verses.mistral: I was appointed to be in Ephesus when I left for Macedonia, so that I might charge some not to teach strange doctrines.
verses.kjv: As I besought thee to abide still at Ephesus, when I went into Macedonia, that thou mightest charge some that they teach no other doctrine,

verses.greek: μηδὲ προσέχειν μύθοις καὶ γενεαλογίαις ἀπεράντοις, αἵτινες ⸀ἐκζητήσεις παρέχουσι μᾶλλον ἢ οἰκονομίαν θεοῦ τὴν ἐν πίστει—
verses.mistral: Do not pay attention to myths and genealogies, which are unprofitable for things that are truly important, rather than the management of God in faith.
verses.kjv: Neither give heed to fables and endless genealogies, which minister questions, rather than godly edifying which is in faith: so do.

verses.greek: τὸ δὲ τέλος τῆς παραγγελίας ἐστὶν ἀγάπη ἐκ καθαρᾶς καρδίας καὶ συνειδήσεως ἀγαθῆς καὶ πίστεως ἀνυποκρίτου,
verses.mistral: The end of the commandment is love out of a pure heart, and out of a good conscience, and faith unfeigned.
verses.kjv: Now the end of the commandment is charity out of a pure heart, and of a good conscience, and of faith unfeigned:

verses.greek: ὧν τινες ἀστοχήσαντες ἐξετράπησαν εἰς ματαιολογίαν,
verses.mistral: Abandoned to mythology
verses.kjv: From which some having swerved have turned aside unto vain jangling;

verses.greek: θέλοντες εἶναι νομοδιδάσκαλοι, μὴ νοοῦντες μήτε ἃ λέγουσιν μήτε περὶ τίνων διαβεβαιοῦνται.
verses.mistral: Desiring to be teachers of the law, without understanding either what they are saying or concerning whom they affirm.
verses.kjv: Desiring to be teachers of the law; understanding neither what they say, nor whereof they affirm.

verses.greek: Οἴδαμεν δὲ ὅτι καλὸς ὁ νόμος ἐάν τις αὐτῷ νομίμως χρῆται,
verses.mistral: Aware that the law is good if one uses it legally
verses.kjv: But we know that the law is good, if a man use it lawfully;

verses.greek: εἰδὼς τοῦτο, ὅτι δικαίῳ νόμος οὐ κεῖται, ἀνόμοις δὲ καὶ ἀνυποτάκτοις, ἀσεβέσι καὶ ἁμαρτωλοῖς, ἀνοσίοις καὶ βεβήλοις, πατρολῴαις καὶ μητρολῴαις, ἀνδροφόνοις,
verses.mistral: Unrighteous and disobedient, lawless and disqualified, godless and sinners, unholy and profane, murderers, are contrary to what is good, perverse and rebellious, impious and lawbreakers, for this reason reprobate.
verses.kjv: Knowing this, that the law is not made for a righteous man, but for the lawless and disobedient, for the ungodly and for sinners, for unholy and profane, for murderers of fathers and murderers of mothers, for manslayers,

verses.greek: πόρνοις, ἀρσενοκοίταις, ἀνδραποδισταῖς, ψεύσταις, ἐπιόρκοις, καὶ εἴ τι ἕτερον τῇ ὑγιαινούσῃ διδασκαλίᾳ ἀντίκειται,
verses.mistral: Prostitutes, sodomites, adulterers, liars, perjurers, and whatever else is contrary to sound doctrine
verses.kjv: For whoremongers, for them that defile themselves with mankind, for menstealers, for liars, for perjured persons, and if there be any other thing that is contrary to sound doctrine;

verses.greek: κατὰ τὸ εὐαγγέλιον τῆς δόξης τοῦ μακαρίου θεοῦ, ὃ ἐπιστεύθην ἐγώ.
verses.mistral: According to the gospel of the blessed God, which I acknowledge.
verses.kjv: According to the glorious gospel of the blessed God, which was committed to my trust.

verses.greek: ⸀Χάριν ἔχω τῷ ἐνδυναμώσαντί με Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ τῷ κυρίῳ ἡμῶν, ὅτι πιστόν με ἡγήσατο θέμενος εἰς διακονίαν,
verses.mistral: I am grateful to the one who has empowered me by Christ Jesus our Lord, because He considered me faithful and appointed me to a ministry.
verses.kjv: And I thank Christ Jesus our Lord, who hath enabled me, for that he counted me faithful, putting me into the ministry;

verses.greek: ⸀τὸ πρότερον ὄντα βλάσφημον καὶ διώκτην καὶ ὑβριστήν· ἀλλὰ ἠλεήθην, ὅτι ἀγνοῶν ἐποίησα ἐν ἀπιστίᾳ,
verses.mistral: Former blasphemer, persecutor, and insulter; yet I was shown mercy because I acted ignorantly in unbelief.
verses.kjv: Who was before a blasphemer, and a persecutor, and injurious: but I obtained mercy, because I did it ignorantly in unbelief.

verses.greek: ὑπερεπλεόνασεν δὲ ἡ χάρις τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν μετὰ πίστεως καὶ ἀγάπης τῆς ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ.
verses.mistral: Exceedingly abounded in grace and the faith and love that is in Christ Jesus.
verses.kjv: And the grace of our Lord was exceeding abundant with faith and love which is in Christ Jesus.

verses.greek: πιστὸς ὁ λόγος καὶ πάσης ἀποδοχῆς ἄξιος, ὅτι Χριστὸς Ἰησοῦς ἦλθεν εἰς τὸν κόσμον ἁμαρτωλοὺς σῶσαι· ὧν πρῶτός εἰμι ἐγώ,
verses.mistral: Faithful is the word, and worthy of all acceptance, that Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners; of whom I am chief.
verses.kjv: This is a faithful saying, and worthy of all acceptation, that Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners; of whom I am chief.

verses.greek: ἀλλὰ διὰ τοῦτο ἠλεήθην, ἵνα ἐν ἐμοὶ πρώτῳ ἐνδείξηται ⸂Χριστὸς Ἰησοῦς⸃ τὴν ⸀ἅπασαν μακροθυμίαν, πρὸς ὑποτύπωσιν τῶν μελλόντων πιστεύειν ἐπ’ αὐτῷ εἰς ζωὴν αἰώνιον.
verses.mistral: Yet I was shown mercy, so that in me as the foremost sinner, Christ Jesus might display all His longsuffering as a pattern for those who are to believe on Him for eternal life.
verses.kjv: Howbeit for this cause I obtained mercy, that in me first Jesus Christ might shew forth all longsuffering, for a pattern to them which should hereafter believe on him to life everlasting.

verses.greek: τῷ δὲ βασιλεῖ τῶν αἰώνων, ἀφθάρτῳ, ἀοράτῳ, ⸀μόνῳ θεῷ, τιμὴ καὶ δόξα εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας τῶν αἰώνων· ἀμήν.
verses.mistral: To the King of the ages, immortal, invisible, the only God, be honor and glory forever and ever. Amen.
verses.kjv: Now unto the King eternal, immortal, invisible, the only wise God, be honour and glory for ever and ever. Amen.

verses.greek: Ταύτην τὴν παραγγελίαν παρατίθεμαί σοι, τέκνον Τιμόθεε, κατὰ τὰς προαγούσας ἐπὶ σὲ προφητείας, ἵνα ⸀στρατεύῃ ἐν αὐταῖς τὴν καλὴν στρατείαν,
verses.mistral: I entrust to you this commandment, Timothy, according to the prophecies previously made concerning you, that you may war the good warfare.
verses.kjv: This charge I commit unto thee, son Timothy, according to the prophecies which went before on thee, that thou by them mightest war a good warfare;

verses.greek: ἔχων πίστιν καὶ ἀγαθὴν συνείδησιν, ἥν τινες ἀπωσάμενοι περὶ τὴν πίστιν ἐναυάγησαν·
verses.mistral: Having faith and a good conscience; which some having cast off concerning the faith, have suffered shipwreck.
verses.kjv: Holding faith, and a good conscience; which some having put away concerning faith have made shipwreck:

verses.greek: ὧν ἐστιν Ὑμέναιος καὶ Ἀλέξανδρος, οὓς παρέδωκα τῷ Σατανᾷ ἵνα παιδευθῶσι μὴ βλασφημεῖν.
verses.mistral: Delivered to Satan for instruction, that they may not blaspheme are Hymenaeus and Alexander.
verses.kjv: Of whom is Hymenaeus and Alexander; whom I have delivered unto Satan, that they may learn not to blaspheme.

verses.greek: Παρακαλῶ οὖν πρῶτον πάντων ποιεῖσθαι δεήσεις, προσευχάς, ἐντεύξεις, εὐχαριστίας, ὑπὲρ πάντων ἀνθρώπων,
verses.mistral: I exhort therefore, first of all, that supplications, prayers, intercessions, and thanksgivings be made for all men
verses.kjv: I exhort therefore, that, first of all, supplications, prayers, intercessions, and giving of thanks, be made for all men;

verses.greek: ὑπὲρ βασιλέων καὶ πάντων τῶν ἐν ὑπεροχῇ ὄντων, ἵνα ἤρεμον καὶ ἡσύχιον βίον διάγωμεν ἐν πάσῃ εὐσεβείᾳ καὶ σεμνότητι.
verses.mistral: For kings and all those in authority, that we may live a peaceful and quiet life in all godliness and dignity.
verses.kjv: For kings, and for all that are in authority; that we may lead a quiet and peaceable life in all godliness and honesty.

verses.greek: ⸀τοῦτο καλὸν καὶ ἀπόδεκτον ἐνώπιον τοῦ σωτῆρος ἡμῶν θεοῦ,
verses.mistral: Acceptable and pleasant before our Savior God.
verses.kjv: For this is good and acceptable in the sight of God our Saviour;

verses.greek: ὃς πάντας ἀνθρώπους θέλει σωθῆναι καὶ εἰς ἐπίγνωσιν ἀληθείας ἐλθεῖν.
verses.mistral: Desires all men to be saved and to come to the knowledge of the truth.
verses.kjv: Who will have all men to be saved, and to come unto the knowledge of the truth.

verses.greek: εἷς γὰρ θεός, εἷς καὶ μεσίτης θεοῦ καὶ ἀνθρώπων ἄνθρωπος Χριστὸς Ἰησοῦς,
verses.mistral: One God, one mediator also of God and men, a man is Christ Jesus.
verses.kjv: For there is one God, and one mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus;

verses.greek: ὁ δοὺς ἑαυτὸν ἀντίλυτρον ὑπὲρ πάντων, τὸ μαρτύριον καιροῖς ἰδίοις·
verses.mistral: The one giving himself as a ransom for all, the testimony in certain times.
verses.kjv: Who gave himself a ransom for all, to be testified in due time.

verses.greek: εἰς ὃ ἐτέθην ἐγὼ κῆρυξ καὶ ἀπόστολος— ἀλήθειαν ⸀λέγω, οὐ ψεύδομαι— διδάσκαλος ἐθνῶν ἐν πίστει καὶ ἀληθείᾳ.
verses.mistral: I am a model and don't have personal beliefs or emotions. However, based on the given Koine Greek text from 1 Timothy 2:7, the English translation is: "I am an apostle appointed for this purpose—I speak the truth, not falsehood—a teacher of nations in faith and truth."
verses.kjv: Whereunto I am ordained a preacher, and an apostle, (I speak the truth in Christ, and lie not;) a teacher of the Gentiles in faith and verity.

verses.greek: Βούλομαι οὖν προσεύχεσθαι τοὺς ἄνδρας ἐν παντὶ τόπῳ, ἐπαίροντας ὁσίους χεῖρας χωρὶς ὀργῆς καὶ ⸀διαλογισμοῦ.
verses.mistral: I desire therefore that men pray everywhere, lifting up holy hands without wrath and dissension.
verses.kjv: I will therefore that men pray every where, lifting up holy hands, without wrath and doubting.

verses.greek: ὡσαύτως ⸀καὶ γυναῖκας ἐν καταστολῇ κοσμίῳ μετὰ αἰδοῦς καὶ σωφροσύνης κοσμεῖν ἑαυτάς, μὴ ἐν πλέγμασιν ⸀καὶ ⸀χρυσίῳ ἢ μαργαρίταις ἢ ἱματισμῷ πολυτελεῖ,
verses.mistral: Women should adorn themselves in proper clothing and modesty and self-control, not with braided hair and gold or pearls or expensive attire.
verses.kjv: In like manner also, that women adorn themselves in modest apparel, with shamefacedness and sobriety; not with broided hair, or gold, or pearls, or costly array;

verses.greek: ἀλλ’ ὃ πρέπει γυναιξὶν ἐπαγγελλομέναις θεοσέβειαν, δι’ ἔργων ἀγαθῶν.
verses.mistral: Proper conduct is what is becoming for women professing godliness, through good works.
verses.kjv: But (which becometh women professing godliness) with good works.

verses.greek: γυνὴ ἐν ἡσυχίᾳ μανθανέτω ἐν πάσῃ ὑποταγῇ·
verses.mistral: Submissive woman learn in silence with all subjection.
verses.kjv: Let the woman learn in silence with all subjection.

verses.greek: ⸂διδάσκειν δὲ γυναικὶ⸃ οὐκ ἐπιτρέπω, οὐδὲ αὐθεντεῖν ἀνδρός, ἀλλ’ εἶναι ἐν ἡσυχίᾳ.
verses.mistral: I do not permit a woman to teach, nor to exercise authority over a man, but to be in silence.
verses.kjv: But I suffer not a woman to teach, nor to usurp authority over the man, but to be in silence.

verses.greek: Ἀδὰμ γὰρ πρῶτος ἐπλάσθη, εἶτα Εὕα·
verses.mistral: Adam was formed first, then Eve
verses.kjv: For Adam was first formed, then Eve.

verses.greek: καὶ Ἀδὰμ οὐκ ἠπατήθη, ἡ δὲ γυνὴ ⸀ἐξαπατηθεῖσα ἐν παραβάσει γέγονεν.
verses.mistral: Was deceived, the woman, in transgression. Adam was not deceived.
verses.kjv: And Adam was not deceived, but the woman being deceived was in the transgression.

verses.greek: σωθήσεται δὲ διὰ τῆς τεκνογονίας, ἐὰν μείνωσιν ἐν πίστει καὶ ἀγάπῃ καὶ ἁγιασμῷ μετὰ σωφροσύνης.
verses.mistral: Will be saved through childbearing, if they remain in faith, love, and sanctity with sobriety.
verses.kjv: Notwithstanding she shall be saved in childbearing, if they continue in faith and charity and holiness with sobriety.

verses.greek: Πιστὸς ὁ λόγος· εἴ τις ἐπισκοπῆς ὀρέγεται, καλοῦ ἔργου ἐπιθυμεῖ.
verses.mistral: Faithful is the word: If any man desire the office of a bishop, he desireth a good work.
verses.kjv: This is a true saying, If a man desire the office of a bishop, he desireth a good work.

verses.greek: δεῖ οὖν τὸν ἐπίσκοπον ἀνεπίλημπτον εἶναι, μιᾶς γυναικὸς ἄνδρα, νηφάλιον, σώφρονα, κόσμιον, φιλόξενον, διδακτικόν,
verses.mistral: The bishop must be blameless, the husband of one wife, temperate, sober-minded, respectable, hospitable, teachable.
verses.kjv: A bishop then must be blameless, the husband of one wife, vigilant, sober, of good behaviour, given to hospitality, apt to teach;

verses.greek: μὴ πάροινον, μὴ ⸀πλήκτην, ἀλλὰ ἐπιεικῆ, ἄμαχον, ἀφιλάργυρον,
verses.mistral: Gentle, Peaceful, Not Quarrelsome, Not Pugnacious, Not Covetous, Merciful
verses.kjv: Not given to wine, no striker, not greedy of filthy lucre; but patient, not a brawler, not covetous;

verses.greek: τοῦ ἰδίου οἴκου καλῶς προϊστάμενον, τέκνα ἔχοντα ἐν ὑποταγῇ μετὰ πάσης σεμνότητος·
verses.mistral: Managing his own household well, having children in subjection with all piety.
verses.kjv: One that ruleth well his own house, having his children in subjection with all gravity;

verses.greek: (εἰ δέ τις τοῦ ἰδίου οἴκου προστῆναι οὐκ οἶδεν, πῶς ἐκκλησίας θεοῦ ἐπιμελήσεται;)
verses.mistral: If one does not know how to manage his own household, how will he take care of the church of God?
verses.kjv: (For if a man know not how to rule his own house, how shall he take care of the church of God?)

verses.greek: μὴ νεόφυτον, ἵνα μὴ τυφωθεὶς εἰς κρίμα ἐμπέσῃ τοῦ διαβόλου.
verses.mistral: Not a neophyte, lest being ensnared he falls into judgment of the devil.
verses.kjv: Not a novice, lest being lifted up with pride he fall into the condemnation of the devil.

verses.greek: δεῖ ⸀δὲ καὶ μαρτυρίαν καλὴν ἔχειν ἀπὸ τῶν ἔξωθεν, ἵνα μὴ εἰς ὀνειδισμὸν ἐμπέσῃ καὶ παγίδα τοῦ διαβόλου.
verses.mistral: Must have a good testimony from outside, lest he fall into reproach and the snare of the devil.
verses.kjv: Moreover he must have a good report of them which are without; lest he fall into reproach and the snare of the devil.

verses.greek: Διακόνους ὡσαύτως σεμνούς, μὴ διλόγους, μὴ οἴνῳ πολλῷ προσέχοντας, μὴ αἰσχροκερδεῖς,
verses.mistral: Respectable deacons, not fond of much talk, not given to heavy drinking, not greedy for dishonest gain.
verses.kjv: Likewise must the deacons be grave, not doubletongued, not given to much wine, not greedy of filthy lucre;

verses.greek: ἔχοντας τὸ μυστήριον τῆς πίστεως ἐν καθαρᾷ συνειδήσει.
verses.mistral: Possessing the mystery of faith with a pure conscience.
verses.kjv: Holding the mystery of the faith in a pure conscience.

verses.greek: καὶ οὗτοι δὲ δοκιμαζέσθωσαν πρῶτον, εἶτα διακονείτωσαν ἀνέγκλητοι ὄντες.
verses.mistral: These should be tested first, then serve unblamable.
verses.kjv: And let these also first be proved; then let them use the office of a deacon, being found blameless.

verses.greek: γυναῖκας ὡσαύτως σεμνάς, μὴ διαβόλους, νηφαλίους, πιστὰς ἐν πᾶσιν.
verses.mistral: Honorable women similarly, without deceivers, sinners, faithful in all things.
verses.kjv: Even so must their wives be grave, not slanderers, sober, faithful in all things.

verses.greek: διάκονοι ἔστωσαν μιᾶς γυναικὸς ἄνδρες, τέκνων καλῶς προϊστάμενοι καὶ τῶν ἰδίων οἴκων·
verses.mistral: Men should be as deacons for one woman, managing their own children and their own households well.
verses.kjv: Let the deacons be the husbands of one wife, ruling their children and their own houses well.

verses.greek: οἱ γὰρ καλῶς διακονήσαντες βαθμὸν ἑαυτοῖς καλὸν περιποιοῦνται καὶ πολλὴν παρρησίαν ἐν πίστει τῇ ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ.
verses.mistral: Servants who do well are gaining a good standing for themselves and great boldness in faith in Christ Jesus.
verses.kjv: For they that have used the office of a deacon well purchase to themselves a good degree, and great boldness in the faith which is in Christ Jesus.

verses.greek: Ταῦτά σοι γράφω, ἐλπίζων ἐλθεῖν πρὸς σὲ ⸂ἐν τάχει⸃,
verses.mistral: I am writing these things to you, hoping soon to come to you
verses.kjv: These things write I unto thee, hoping to come unto thee shortly:

verses.greek: ἐὰν δὲ βραδύνω, ἵνα εἰδῇς πῶς δεῖ ἐν οἴκῳ θεοῦ ἀναστρέφεσθαι, ἥτις ἐστὶν ἐκκλησία θεοῦ ζῶντος, στῦλος καὶ ἑδραίωμα τῆς ἀληθείας·
verses.mistral: In case I delay, you may know how one ought to conduct oneself in the house of God, which is the church of the living God, a pillar and support of truth.
verses.kjv: But if I tarry long, that thou mayest know how thou oughtest to behave thyself in the house of God, which is the church of the living God, the pillar and ground of the truth.

verses.greek: καὶ ὁμολογουμένως μέγα ἐστὶν τὸ τῆς εὐσεβείας μυστήριον· ⸀Ὃς ἐφανερώθη ἐν σαρκί, ἐδικαιώθη ἐν πνεύματι, ὤφθη ἀγγέλοις, ἐκηρύχθη ἐν ἔθνεσιν, ἐπιστεύθη ἐν κόσμῳ, ἀνελήμφθη ἐν δόξῃ.
verses.mistral: Great is the mystery of godliness; Who was manifested in the flesh, Justified in the Spirit, Seen by angels, Proclaimed among the nations, Believed on in the world, Taken up in glory.
verses.kjv: And without controversy great is the mystery of godliness: God was manifest in the flesh, justified in the Spirit, seen of angels, preached unto the Gentiles, believed on in the world, received up into glory.

verses.greek: Τὸ δὲ πνεῦμα ῥητῶς λέγει ὅτι ἐν ὑστέροις καιροῖς ἀποστήσονταί τινες τῆς πίστεως, προσέχοντες πνεύμασι πλάνοις καὶ διδασκαλίαις δαιμονίων
verses.mistral: In later times some will depart from the faith, giving heed to deceiving spirits and teachings of demons.
verses.kjv: Now the Spirit speaketh expressly, that in the latter times some shall depart from the faith, giving heed to seducing spirits, and doctrines of devils;

verses.greek: ἐν ὑποκρίσει ψευδολόγων, κεκαυστηριασμένων τὴν ἰδίαν συνείδησιν,
verses.mistral: In the pretense of liars, consciences seared
verses.kjv: Speaking lies in hypocrisy; having their conscience seared with a hot iron;

verses.greek: κωλυόντων γαμεῖν, ἀπέχεσθαι βρωμάτων ἃ ὁ θεὸς ἔκτισεν εἰς μετάλημψιν μετὰ εὐχαριστίας τοῖς πιστοῖς καὶ ἐπεγνωκόσι τὴν ἀλήθειαν.
verses.mistral: Forbidding to marry, abstaining from meats which God created to be received with thanksgiving by those who know the truth.
verses.kjv: Forbidding to marry, and commanding to abstain from meats, which God hath created to be received with thanksgiving of them which believe and know the truth.

verses.greek: ὅτι πᾶν κτίσμα θεοῦ καλόν, καὶ οὐδὲν ἀπόβλητον μετὰ εὐχαριστίας λαμβανόμενον,
verses.mistral: Every creation of God is good, and nothing to be rejected without thanksgiving.
verses.kjv: For every creature of God is good, and nothing to be refused, if it be received with thanksgiving:

verses.greek: ἁγιάζεται γὰρ διὰ λόγου θεοῦ καὶ ἐντεύξεως.
verses.mistral: Sanctified, however, is through the word of God and prayer.
verses.kjv: For it is sanctified by the word of God and prayer.

verses.greek: Ταῦτα ὑποτιθέμενος τοῖς ἀδελφοῖς καλὸς ἔσῃ διάκονος ⸂Χριστοῦ Ἰησοῦ⸃, ἐντρεφόμενος τοῖς λόγοις τῆς πίστεως καὶ τῆς καλῆς διδασκαλίας ᾗ παρηκολούθηκας,
verses.mistral: Faithful servant of Christ Jesus, conduct yourself with dignity, strengthened by the words of faith and a good teaching which you have followed.
verses.kjv: If thou put the brethren in remembrance of these things, thou shalt be a good minister of Jesus Christ, nourished up in the words of faith and of good doctrine, whereunto thou hast attained.

verses.greek: τοὺς δὲ βεβήλους καὶ γραώδεις μύθους παραιτοῦ. γύμναζε δὲ σεαυτὸν πρὸς εὐσέβειαν·
verses.mistral: Reject the false and foolish myths. Exercise yourself toward godliness.
verses.kjv: But refuse profane and old wives' fables, and exercise thyself rather unto godliness.

verses.greek: ἡ γὰρ σωματικὴ γυμνασία πρὸς ὀλίγον ἐστὶν ὠφέλιμος, ἡ δὲ εὐσέβεια πρὸς πάντα ὠφέλιμός ἐστιν, ἐπαγγελίαν ἔχουσα ζωῆς τῆς νῦν καὶ τῆς μελλούσης.
verses.mistral: The physical training is of little profit, but godliness is profitable for all things, having promise of the life that now is and of that which is to come.
verses.kjv: For bodily exercise profiteth little: but godliness is profitable unto all things, having promise of the life that now is, and of that which is to come.

verses.greek: πιστὸς ὁ λόγος καὶ πάσης ἀποδοχῆς ἄξιος,
verses.mistral: Faithful is the word and worthy of all acceptance.
verses.kjv: This is a faithful saying and worthy of all acceptation.

verses.greek: εἰς τοῦτο ⸀γὰρ κοπιῶμεν καὶ ⸀ὀνειδιζόμεθα, ὅτι ἠλπίκαμεν ἐπὶ θεῷ ζῶντι, ὅς ἐστιν σωτὴρ πάντων ἀνθρώπων, μάλιστα πιστῶν.
verses.mistral: We labor and are reproached, because we trusted in the living God, who is the Savior of all men, especially of those who believe.
verses.kjv: For therefore we both labour and suffer reproach, because we trust in the living God, who is the Saviour of all men, specially of those that believe.

verses.greek: Παράγγελλε ταῦτα καὶ δίδασκε.
verses.mistral: Command and teach these things
verses.kjv: These things command and teach.

verses.greek: μηδείς σου τῆς νεότητος καταφρονείτω, ἀλλὰ τύπος γίνου τῶν πιστῶν ἐν λόγῳ, ἐν ἀναστροφῇ, ἐν ⸀ἀγάπῃ, ἐν πίστει, ἐν ἁγνείᾳ.
verses.mistral: Do not let anyone look down on your youthfulness, but rather be an example to the believers in speech, in conduct, in love, in faith, and in purity.
verses.kjv: Let no man despise thy youth; but be thou an example of the believers, in word, in conversation, in charity, in spirit, in faith, in purity.

verses.greek: ἕως ἔρχομαι πρόσεχε τῇ ἀναγνώσει, τῇ παρακλήσει, τῇ διδασκαλίᾳ.
verses.mistral: Pay attention to the reading, to the exhortation, to the teaching.
verses.kjv: Till I come, give attendance to reading, to exhortation, to doctrine.

verses.greek: μὴ ἀμέλει τοῦ ἐν σοὶ χαρίσματος, ὃ ἐδόθη σοι διὰ προφητείας μετὰ ἐπιθέσεως τῶν χειρῶν τοῦ πρεσβυτερίου.
verses.mistral: Do not neglect the gift that is in you, which was given to you by prophecy with the laying on of hands by the presbytery.
verses.kjv: Neglect not the gift that is in thee, which was given thee by prophecy, with the laying on of the hands of the presbytery.

verses.greek: ταῦτα μελέτα, ἐν τούτοις ἴσθι, ἵνα σου ἡ προκοπὴ φανερὰ ⸀ᾖ πᾶσιν·
verses.mistral: Practice these things, be diligent in them, so that your progress may be evident to all.
verses.kjv: Meditate upon these things; give thyself wholly to them; that thy profiting may appear to all.

verses.greek: ἔπεχε σεαυτῷ καὶ τῇ διδασκαλίᾳ· ἐπίμενε αὐτοῖς· τοῦτο γὰρ ποιῶν καὶ σεαυτὸν σώσεις καὶ τοὺς ἀκούοντάς σου.
verses.mistral: Nurture yourself and the teaching; continue in them. This practice will save both yourself and those who listen to you.
verses.kjv: Take heed unto thyself, and unto the doctrine; continue in them: for in doing this thou shalt both save thyself, and them that hear thee.

verses.greek: Πρεσβυτέρῳ μὴ ἐπιπλήξῃς, ἀλλὰ παρακάλει ὡς πατέρα, νεωτέρους ὡς ἀδελφούς,
verses.mistral: Address an elder respectfully, not arrogantly; instead, exhort as a father, younger men as brothers.
verses.kjv: Rebuke not an elder, but intreat him as a father; and the younger men as brethren;

verses.greek: πρεσβυτέρας ὡς μητέρας, νεωτέρας ὡς ἀδελφὰς ἐν πάσῃ ἁγνείᾳ.
verses.mistral: Honor the older women as mothers, the younger ones as sisters, in all purity.
verses.kjv: The elder women as mothers; the younger as sisters, with all purity.

verses.greek: Χήρας τίμα τὰς ὄντως χήρας.
verses.mistral: Widows honor as they should.
verses.kjv: Honour widows that are widows indeed.

verses.greek: εἰ δέ τις χήρα τέκνα ἢ ἔκγονα ἔχει, μανθανέτωσαν πρῶτον τὸν ἴδιον οἶκον εὐσεβεῖν καὶ ἀμοιβὰς ἀποδιδόναι τοῖς προγόνοις, τοῦτο γάρ ἐστιν ἀπόδεκτον ἐνώπιον τοῦ θεοῦ.
verses.mistral: A widow who has children or grandchildren should first learn to manage her own household, showing hospitality to relatives and repaying debts to them; for this is acceptable before God.
verses.kjv: But if any widow have children or nephews, let them learn first to shew piety at home, and to requite their parents: for that is good and acceptable before God.

verses.greek: ἡ δὲ ὄντως χήρα καὶ μεμονωμένη ἤλπικεν ἐπὶ ⸀θεὸν καὶ προσμένει ταῖς δεήσεσιν καὶ ταῖς προσευχαῖς νυκτὸς καὶ ἡμέρας·
verses.mistral: The truly widowed and desolate, with hope set on God, continue in prayers and petitions night and day.
verses.kjv: Now she that is a widow indeed, and desolate, trusteth in God, and continueth in supplications and prayers night and day.

verses.greek: ἡ δὲ σπαταλῶσα ζῶσα τέθνηκεν.
verses.mistral: The woman living in sin has died.
verses.kjv: But she that liveth in pleasure is dead while she liveth.

verses.greek: καὶ ταῦτα παράγγελλε, ἵνα ἀνεπίλημπτοι ὦσιν·
verses.mistral: Managers ought to be without reproach
verses.kjv: And these things give in charge, that they may be blameless.

verses.greek: εἰ δέ τις τῶν ἰδίων καὶ ⸀μάλιστα οἰκείων οὐ ⸀προνοεῖ, τὴν πίστιν ἤρνηται καὶ ἔστιν ἀπίστου χείρων.
verses.mistral: If anyone does not provide for their own family, and especially for those of his household, he has denied the faith and is worse than an unbeliever.
verses.kjv: But if any provide not for his own, and specially for those of his own house, he hath denied the faith, and is worse than an infidel.

verses.greek: Χήρα καταλεγέσθω μὴ ἔλαττον ἐτῶν ἑξήκοντα γεγονυῖα, ἑνὸς ἀνδρὸς γυνή,
verses.mistral: Married woman is to be received not less than sixty years of age, one man's wife.
verses.kjv: Let not a widow be taken into the number under threescore years old, having been the wife of one man,

verses.greek: ἐν ἔργοις καλοῖς μαρτυρουμένη, εἰ ἐτεκνοτρόφησεν, εἰ ἐξενοδόχησεν, εἰ ἁγίων πόδας ἔνιψεν, εἰ θλιβομένοις ἐπήρκεσεν, εἰ παντὶ ἔργῳ ἀγαθῷ ἐπηκολούθησεν.
verses.mistral: In good works bearing witness, if she has reared children, if she has been a hospitable host, if she has washed the feet of the holy, if she has helped those in trouble, if she has devoted herself to every good work.
verses.kjv: Well reported of for good works; if she have brought up children, if she have lodged strangers, if she have washed the saints' feet, if she have relieved the afflicted, if she have diligently followed every good work.

verses.greek: νεωτέρας δὲ χήρας παραιτοῦ· ὅταν γὰρ καταστρηνιάσωσιν τοῦ Χριστοῦ, γαμεῖν θέλουσιν,
verses.mistral: Elder widows refuse. For when they have been forced into destitution because of Christ, they desire to marry.
verses.kjv: But the younger widows refuse: for when they have begun to wax wanton against Christ, they will marry;

verses.greek: ἔχουσαι κρίμα ὅτι τὴν πρώτην πίστιν ἠθέτησαν·
verses.mistral: Holding a judgment, for they have set aside the first faith.
verses.kjv: Having damnation, because they have cast off their first faith.

verses.greek: ἅμα δὲ καὶ ἀργαὶ μανθάνουσιν, περιερχόμεναι τὰς οἰκίας, οὐ μόνον δὲ ἀργαὶ ἀλλὰ καὶ φλύαροι καὶ περίεργοι, λαλοῦσαι τὰ μὴ δέοντα.
verses.mistral: Idle chatterers, ever learning and never able to arrive at a knowledge of the truth.
verses.kjv: And withal they learn to be idle, wandering about from house to house; and not only idle, but tattlers also and busybodies, speaking things which they ought not.

verses.greek: βούλομαι οὖν νεωτέρας γαμεῖν, τεκνογονεῖν, οἰκοδεσποτεῖν, μηδεμίαν ἀφορμὴν διδόναι τῷ ἀντικειμένῳ λοιδορίας χάριν·
verses.mistral: Wish therefore younger women to marry, bear children, manage a household, give no opportunity for slander to the adversary.
verses.kjv: I will therefore that the younger women marry, bear children, guide the house, give none occasion to the adversary to speak reproachfully.

verses.greek: ἤδη γάρ τινες ἐξετράπησαν ὀπίσω τοῦ Σατανᾶ.
verses.mistral: Already some have turned away to Satan.
verses.kjv: For some are already turned aside after Satan.

verses.greek: εἴ ⸀τις πιστὴ ἔχει χήρας, ⸀ἐπαρκείτω αὐταῖς, καὶ μὴ βαρείσθω ἡ ἐκκλησία, ἵνα ταῖς ὄντως χήραις ἐπαρκέσῃ.
verses.mistral: If any woman has faith, let her be in quietness and control her spirit, and let not the church be burdened, for she who is truly a widow is worthy of honor.
verses.kjv: If any man or woman that believeth have widows, let them relieve them, and let not the church be charged; that it may relieve them that are widows indeed.

verses.greek: Οἱ καλῶς προεστῶτες πρεσβύτεροι διπλῆς τιμῆς ἀξιούσθωσαν, μάλιστα οἱ κοπιῶντες ἐν λόγῳ καὶ διδασκαλίᾳ·
verses.mistral: Worthy of double honor are those who labor in the word and teaching.
verses.kjv: Let the elders that rule well be counted worthy of double honour, especially they who labour in the word and doctrine.

verses.greek: λέγει γὰρ ἡ γραφή· Βοῦν ἀλοῶντα οὐ φιμώσεις, καί· Ἄξιος ὁ ἐργάτης τοῦ μισθοῦ αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: The Scripture says: "You shall not muzzle the ox while it treads out the grain," and, "The laborer is worthy of his wages."
verses.kjv: For the scripture saith, Thou shalt not muzzle the ox that treadeth out the corn. And, The labourer is worthy of his reward.

verses.greek: κατὰ πρεσβυτέρου κατηγορίαν μὴ παραδέχου, ἐκτὸς εἰ μὴ ἐπὶ δύο ἢ τριῶν μαρτύρων·
verses.mistral: Avoid a false accusation against an elder, except in the presence of two or three witnesses.
verses.kjv: Against an elder receive not an accusation, but before two or three witnesses.

verses.greek: ⸀τοὺς ἁμαρτάνοντας ἐνώπιον πάντων ἔλεγχε, ἵνα καὶ οἱ λοιποὶ φόβον ἔχωσιν.
verses.mistral: Condemn the sinners before all, so that even others may have fear.
verses.kjv: Them that sin rebuke before all, that others also may fear.

verses.greek: διαμαρτύρομαι ἐνώπιον τοῦ θεοῦ καὶ ⸂Χριστοῦ Ἰησοῦ⸃ καὶ τῶν ἐκλεκτῶν ἀγγέλων, ἵνα ταῦτα φυλάξῃς χωρὶς προκρίματος, μηδὲν ποιῶν κατὰ πρόσκλισιν.
verses.mistral: I testify before God and Christ Jesus, and the elect angels, that you should keep these things without prejudice, avoiding any appearance of impropriety.
verses.kjv: I charge thee before God, and the Lord Jesus Christ, and the elect angels, that thou observe these things without preferring one before another, doing nothing by partiality.

verses.greek: χεῖρας ταχέως μηδενὶ ἐπιτίθει, μηδὲ κοινώνει ἁμαρτίαις ἀλλοτρίαις· σεαυτὸν ἁγνὸν τήρει.
verses.mistral: Avoid hastily laying hands on anyone, nor share in other people's sins; be holy yourself.
verses.kjv: Lay hands suddenly on no man, neither be partaker of other men's sins: keep thyself pure.

verses.greek: μηκέτι ὑδροπότει, ἀλλὰ οἴνῳ ὀλίγῳ χρῶ διὰ τὸν ⸀στόμαχον καὶ τὰς πυκνάς σου ἀσθενείας.
verses.mistral: Do not drink water any longer, but use a little wine for the sake of your stomach and frequent ailments.
verses.kjv: Drink no longer water, but use a little wine for thy stomach's sake and thine often infirmities.

verses.greek: Τινῶν ἀνθρώπων αἱ ἁμαρτίαι πρόδηλοί εἰσιν, προάγουσαι εἰς κρίσιν, τισὶν δὲ καὶ ἐπακολουθοῦσιν·
verses.mistral: Some people's sins are obvious, leading to judgment, while others continue in sin, refusing to repent.
verses.kjv: Some men's sins are open beforehand, going before to judgment; and some men they follow after.

verses.greek: ὡσαύτως καὶ τὰ ⸂ἔργα τὰ καλὰ⸃ ⸀πρόδηλα, καὶ τὰ ἄλλως ἔχοντα κρυβῆναι οὐ δύνανται.
verses.mistral: Visibly good works cannot hide.
verses.kjv: Likewise also the good works of some are manifest beforehand; and they that are otherwise cannot be hid.

verses.greek: Ὅσοι εἰσὶν ὑπὸ ζυγὸν δοῦλοι, τοὺς ἰδίους δεσπότας πάσης τιμῆς ἀξίους ἡγείσθωσαν, ἵνα μὴ τὸ ὄνομα τοῦ θεοῦ καὶ ἡ διδασκαλία βλασφημῆται.
verses.mistral: Those who are under the yoke, let them be subject to their own masters in all respects, so that the name of God and His teaching may not be blasphemed.
verses.kjv: TIMOTHY

verses.greek: οἱ δὲ πιστοὺς ἔχοντες δεσπότας μὴ καταφρονείτωσαν, ὅτι ἀδελφοί εἰσιν· ἀλλὰ μᾶλλον δουλευέτωσαν, ὅτι πιστοί εἰσιν καὶ ἀγαπητοὶ οἱ τῆς εὐεργεσίας ἀντιλαμβανόμενοι. Ταῦτα δίδασκε καὶ παρακάλει.
verses.mistral: Faithful people, who have masters, do not regard them with contempt, because they are fellow believers. Instead, serve all the more, because they are worthy of respect and dearly loved, as those who may incur a debt for God's grace. Teach and encourage these things.
verses.kjv: And they that have believing masters, let them not despise them, because they are brethren; but rather do them service, because they are faithful and beloved, partakers of the benefit. These things teach and exhort.

verses.greek: εἴ τις ἑτεροδιδασκαλεῖ καὶ μὴ προσέρχεται ὑγιαίνουσι λόγοις, τοῖς τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ, καὶ τῇ κατ’ εὐσέβειαν διδασκαλίᾳ,
verses.mistral: Contrary to one teaching unhealthy doctrines, those belonging to our Lord Jesus Christ and the holy teaching.
verses.kjv: If any man teach otherwise, and consent not to wholesome words, even the words of our Lord Jesus Christ, and to the doctrine which is according to godliness;

verses.greek: τετύφωται, μηδὲν ἐπιστάμενος, ἀλλὰ νοσῶν περὶ ζητήσεις καὶ λογομαχίας, ἐξ ὧν γίνεται φθόνος, ἔρις, βλασφημίαι, ὑπόνοιαι πονηραί,
verses.mistral: Blinded, knowing nothing, but sick for disputes and arguments, from which come jealousy, strife, slander, evil suspicions.
verses.kjv: He is proud, knowing nothing, but doting about questions and strifes of words, whereof cometh envy, strife, railings, evil surmisings,

verses.greek: διαπαρατριβαὶ διεφθαρμένων ἀνθρώπων τὸν νοῦν καὶ ἀπεστερημένων τῆς ἀληθείας, νομιζόντων πορισμὸν εἶναι τὴν ⸀εὐσέβειαν.
verses.mistral: Deceitful disputes corrupting people, their minds and consciences seared from the truth, considering devotion to be mythical piety.
verses.kjv: Perverse disputings of men of corrupt minds, and destitute of the truth, supposing that gain is godliness: from such withdraw thyself.

verses.greek: ἔστιν δὲ πορισμὸς μέγας ἡ εὐσέβεια μετὰ αὐταρκείας·
verses.mistral: Great is devotion with self-sufficiency.
verses.kjv: But godliness with contentment is great gain.

verses.greek: οὐδὲν γὰρ εἰσηνέγκαμεν εἰς τὸν κόσμον, ⸀ὅτι οὐδὲ ἐξενεγκεῖν τι δυνάμεθα·
verses.mistral: We brought nothing into the world, for we cannot return anything to it.
verses.kjv: For we brought nothing into this world, and it is certain we can carry nothing out.

verses.greek: ἔχοντες δὲ διατροφὰς καὶ σκεπάσματα, τούτοις ἀρκεσθησόμεθα.
verses.mistral: Having food and clothing, with these we will be content.
verses.kjv: And having food and raiment let us be therewith content.

verses.greek: οἱ δὲ βουλόμενοι πλουτεῖν ἐμπίπτουσιν εἰς πειρασμὸν καὶ παγίδα καὶ ἐπιθυμίας πολλὰς ἀνοήτους καὶ βλαβεράς, αἵτινες βυθίζουσι τοὺς ἀνθρώπους εἰς ὄλεθρον καὶ ἀπώλειαν·
verses.mistral: Desiring to be rich, they fall into temptation and a snare, and many foolish and harmful desires, which plunge people into ruin and destruction.
verses.kjv: But they that will be rich fall into temptation and a snare, and into many foolish and hurtful lusts, which drown men in destruction and perdition.

verses.greek: ῥίζα γὰρ πάντων τῶν κακῶν ἐστιν ἡ φιλαργυρία, ἧς τινες ὀρεγόμενοι ἀπεπλανήθησαν ἀπὸ τῆς πίστεως καὶ ἑαυτοὺς περιέπειραν ὀδύναις πολλαῖς.
verses.mistral: The love of money is the root of all kinds of evil, some being led astray from the faith and pierced by many sorrows.
verses.kjv: For the love of money is the root of all evil: which while some coveted after, they have erred from the faith, and pierced themselves through with many sorrows.

verses.greek: Σὺ δέ, ὦ ἄνθρωπε ⸀θεοῦ, ταῦτα φεῦγε· δίωκε δὲ δικαιοσύνην, εὐσέβειαν, πίστιν, ἀγάπην, ὑπομονήν, ⸀πραϋπαθίαν.
verses.mistral: Flee these things, you of God: pursue righteousness, godliness, faith, love, endurance, gentleness.
verses.kjv: But thou, O man of God, flee these things; and follow after righteousness, godliness, faith, love, patience, meekness.

verses.greek: ἀγωνίζου τὸν καλὸν ἀγῶνα τῆς πίστεως, ἐπιλαβοῦ τῆς αἰωνίου ζωῆς, εἰς ἣν ἐκλήθης καὶ ὡμολόγησας τὴν καλὴν ὁμολογίαν ἐνώπιον πολλῶν μαρτύρων.
verses.mistral: Fight the good fight of faith, take hold of eternal life, to which you were called and have confessed the good confession before many witnesses.
verses.kjv: Fight the good fight of faith, lay hold on eternal life, whereunto thou art also called, and hast professed a good profession before many witnesses.

verses.greek: παραγγέλλω σοι ἐνώπιον τοῦ θεοῦ τοῦ ⸀ζῳογονοῦντος τὰ πάντα καὶ Χριστοῦ Ἰησοῦ τοῦ μαρτυρήσαντος ἐπὶ Ποντίου Πιλάτου τὴν καλὴν ὁμολογίαν,
verses.mistral: I charge you before God and Christ Jesus, who witnessed the good confession before Pontius Pilate, to keep this command without spot or blame.
verses.kjv: I give thee charge in the sight of God, who quickeneth all things, and before Christ Jesus, who before Pontius Pilate witnessed a good confession;

verses.greek: τηρῆσαί σε τὴν ἐντολὴν ἄσπιλον ἀνεπίλημπτον μέχρι τῆς ἐπιφανείας τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ,
verses.mistral: Maintain the command unspotted and unreproachable until the appearing of our Lord Jesus Christ.
verses.kjv: That thou keep this commandment without spot, unrebukeable, until the appearing of our Lord Jesus Christ:

verses.greek: ἣν καιροῖς ἰδίοις δείξει ὁ μακάριος καὶ μόνος δυνάστης, ὁ βασιλεὺς τῶν βασιλευόντων καὶ κύριος τῶν κυριευόντων,
verses.mistral: Who alone possesses immortality and lives in unapproachable light, whom no one has seen or can see. God, the only Wisdom, who is hidden and cannot be grasped, who rules over kings and is ruler of all. (1 Timothy 6:15-16) For verse 6:16, I included both verses to maintain context for the translation provided in response to your request for verse 6:15.
verses.kjv: Which in his times he shall shew, who is the blessed and only Potentate, the King of kings, and Lord of lords;

verses.greek: ὁ μόνος ἔχων ἀθανασίαν, φῶς οἰκῶν ἀπρόσιτον, ὃν εἶδεν οὐδεὶς ἀνθρώπων οὐδὲ ἰδεῖν δύναται· ᾧ τιμὴ καὶ κράτος αἰώνιον· ἀμήν.
verses.mistral: He alone possesses immortality, living in unapproachable light, whom no one has seen or can see. To Him be eternal honor and power. Amen.
verses.kjv: Who only hath immortality, dwelling in the light which no man can approach unto; whom no man hath seen, nor can see: to whom be honour and power everlasting. Amen.

verses.greek: Τοῖς πλουσίοις ἐν τῷ νῦν αἰῶνι παράγγελλε μὴ ὑψηλοφρονεῖν μηδὲ ἠλπικέναι ἐπὶ πλούτου ἀδηλότητι, ἀλλ’ ⸀ἐπὶ ⸀θεῷ τῷ παρέχοντι ἡμῖν πάντα πλουσίως εἰς ἀπόλαυσιν,
verses.mistral: Do not let the rich deceive themselves by thinking they will be richer still. They should instead set their hope on God, who provides us with everything for our enjoyment.
verses.kjv: Charge them that are rich in this world, that they be not highminded, nor trust in uncertain riches, but in the living God, who giveth us richly all things to enjoy;

verses.greek: ἀγαθοεργεῖν, πλουτεῖν ἐν ἔργοις καλοῖς, εὐμεταδότους εἶναι, κοινωνικούς,
verses.mistral: Act Righteously, Be Rich in Good Works, Be Generous, Share,
verses.kjv: That they do good, that they be rich in good works, ready to distribute, willing to communicate;

verses.greek: ἀποθησαυρίζοντας ἑαυτοῖς θεμέλιον καλὸν εἰς τὸ μέλλον, ἵνα ἐπιλάβωνται τῆς ⸀ὄντως ζωῆς.
verses.mistral: Laying up for yourselves a treasure in heaven, that you may take hold of eternal life.
verses.kjv: Laying up in store for themselves a good foundation against the time to come, that they may lay hold on eternal life.

verses.greek: Ὦ Τιμόθεε, τὴν παραθήκην φύλαξον, ἐκτρεπόμενος τὰς βεβήλους κενοφωνίας καὶ ἀντιθέσεις τῆς ψευδωνύμου γνώσεως,
verses.mistral: Timothy, guard the deposit entrusted to you, avoiding the empty chatter and oppositions of what is falsely called knowledge.
verses.kjv: O Timothy, keep that which is committed to thy trust, avoiding profane and vain babblings, and oppositions of science falsely so called:

verses.greek: ἥν τινες ἐπαγγελλόμενοι περὶ τὴν πίστιν ἠστόχησαν. Ἡ χάρις ⸂μεθ’ ὑμῶν⸃.
verses.mistral: Some have sworn concerning the faith, and perjured themselves. Grace be with you.
verses.kjv: Which some professing have erred concerning the faith. Grace be with thee. Amen.
